summary: MVP features tested. CRITICAL BUG found: Wallet analysis returns 500 error due to datetime comparison issue in check_usage_limit() function (server.py line 244-247). Auth flows (login, password reset) working. UI simplification verified (Help/Support hidden, Import CSV and Chain of Custody visible for paid users).

issues.description: Wallet analysis returns 500 Internal Server Error due to TypeError in check_usage_limit(): 'can't subtract offset-naive and offset-aware datetimes'. The datetime.fromisoformat() at line 244 returns a naive datetime if the stored ISO string doesn't have timezone info, but datetime.now(timezone.utc) returns an aware datetime.
issues.reproduction:
1. Login as any user (e.g., mobiletest@test.com / test123456)
2. Enter wallet address: 0xd8dA6BF26964aF9D7eEd9e03E53415D37aA96045
3. Click Analyze
4. See error 'Failed to analyze wallet'
Or via curl: POST /api/wallet/analyze with valid auth token
issues.fix_suggestion: In server.py line 244, ensure the parsed datetime is timezone-aware: if isinstance(last_reset, str): last_reset = datetime.fromisoformat(last_reset.replace('Z', '+00:00')); if last_reset.tzinfo is None: last_reset = last_reset.replace(tzinfo=timezone.utc)

action_items: FIX CRITICAL: Fix datetime comparison bug in server.py check_usage_limit() function (line 244-247). The last_reset datetime parsed from ISO string needs to be timezone-aware.

rca_of_issue:
Root Cause: In server.py check_usage_limit() function, line 244 uses datetime.fromisoformat(last_reset) which may return a naive datetime (without timezone info) if the stored ISO string doesn't include timezone. However, line 246 compares it with datetime.now(timezone.utc) which is timezone-aware. This causes TypeError: can't subtract offset-naive and offset-aware datetimes.

Fix: Ensure the parsed datetime is timezone-aware by adding timezone info if missing:
if last_reset.tzinfo is None:
    last_reset = last_reset.replace(tzinfo=timezone.utc)
